evals.name: write-mode-like-post
evals.prompt: 帮我写一个 spec：用户可以给帖子点赞，同一用户对同一帖子只能点一次。
evals.expected_output: A markdown spec file at docs/specs/{feature}.md with Scope (Goal + Out of Scope), AC (using '[ ] **AC-NN**' format), and Verification (BDD scenarios + TDD pointers). No UI details should appear in AC.
expectations: Output is saved to a path under docs/specs/
Spec has a top-level feature title and a Goal section (or equivalent one-sentence goal)
Spec has an 'Out of Scope' or Non-goals section with at least one entry
Spec uses '[ ] **AC-NN**' format for acceptance criteria
AC explicitly covers the uniqueness constraint: one like per user per post
AC covers the happy path (a user likes a post)
Spec has a Verification section with BDD Gherkin scenarios OR TDD pointers
BDD scenarios (if present) reference a specific AC (e.g., '# Maps to AC-01')
No UI details (colors, pixel values, button labels) appear in AC text
All AC markers are '[ ]' (not yet implemented) since this is a fresh spec

evals.name: update-mode-ac-sync
evals.prompt: 开发完了，请同步一下刚才那个点赞功能的 spec 状态。
evals.expected_output: Updated spec with AC-01 marked [x] (implemented + tested), AC-02 marked [~] (implemented but untested — flagged as test debt), AC-03 remains [ ]. Plus an Update Report summarizing changes.
expectations: AC-01 is marked [x] because both implementation and test exist
AC-02 is marked [~] because implementation exists but test is missing
AC-03 remains [ ] because implementation is missing
The output explicitly flags AC-02 as test debt (not silently marks [x])
Original AC descriptions are preserved — only the state marker changes
The Update Report has a 'Test Coverage Gaps' or equivalent section listing AC-02
The skill does not invent new AC that aren't supported by the diff

evals.name: audit-mode-flawed-docs
evals.expected_output: A summary-first audit report grouped by affected file. Does not modify any files.
expectations: Report title includes pass count (e.g., 'N/M passed')
Failures are grouped by the affected file, not a wide matrix where every check is a column
Missing 'Out of Scope' section in specs/buggy.md is flagged
An [~] untested AC is flagged as test debt
Missing boundary ('Does NOT own') section in a module contract is flagged
Orphan test (test with no matching AC) is flagged if present
No files are modified during the audit
Passed checks are collapsed (e.g., in a <details> block) rather than shown in full
